name: Lemon Crepes
recipeInstructions:
Info: Good Food magazine, March 1986 posted by Perry
Lowell, INTERCOOK Echo, Feb. '92
Preparation: 50 minutes Cook: 15 minutes Plus: 2
hours to chill batter
Crepe Batter:
1. Make crepe batter: Whisk egg and milk lightly together in a medium mixing bowl. Add flour, sugar, lemon zest, and salt; whisk until smooth. Refrigerate covered at least 2 hours or overnight.
2. One hour before serving, make lemon sauce: Heat water and sugar in a heavy medium saucepan until sugar dissolves. Add lemon slices and simmer for 30 minutes. Cool to room temperature.
3. Make crepes: Coat the crepe pan with a thin layer of butter or oil on a 6-inch nonstick skillet. Heat pan over medium-high heat. Pour in 2 tablespoons of the crepe batter and quickly tilt the pan to spread the batter evenly. Cook until the bottom is golden and the edges have pulled away from the side of the pan, about 3 minutes. Turn the crepe and cook the second side about 1 minute. Let cool on a plate and repeat with remaining batter to make 8 crepes in all. (Crepes can be cooked in advance and refrigerated or frozen, tightly wrapped. Warm in an oven set to 300 degrees before serving.)
4. Just before serving, make cream filling: Beat cream, sugar, and vanilla extract until stiff peaks form.
5. Place 2 crepes, golden side down, on each dessert plate. Spoon the cream filling onto each crepe and roll up, folding in the edges and placing the seam side down on the plates. Pour 1/4 cup lemon sauce over each serving, and serve at once.
